description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a square, slightly convex glass display framed by a thick, off-white plastic bezel. The front features a pale blue screen with a simulated desktop interface, and a single round button with a green indicator light is located on the lower right bezel. The monitor's body is bulky and angular, with a deep, tapering rear housing that extends back from the screen, typical of late 1990s or early 2000s designs. The base is a wide, circular pedestal that provides stability, and the overall construction appears robust and utilitarian. No visible ports or branding are present, and the rear and sides are plain, smooth plastic.
description: A bulky, off-white CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, convex glass screen, and a wide circular base. The front features a single button and a simulated desktop display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass screen, thick off-white bezel, a green power button on the lower right, and a simulated desktop interface.
description_view_1: Rear angled view displaying the deep, angular plastic housing and the wide circular base, with no visible ports or branding.
description_view_2: Top-down view highlighting the monitor's rectangular shape and the circular base, with the screen and bezel edges visible.
description_view_3: Rear angled view similar to view 1, emphasizing the monitor's depth and the smooth, unadorned plastic surface.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, off-white plastic with a flat, angular profile and slightly rounded corners. It is uniform in color and texture, with no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: Wide, circular pedestal base made of matching off-white plastic, providing stable support with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A single round button with a green indicator light is located on the lower right of the front bezel, separated from any other controls.
